free_text: A systematic investigation of (n,t) reactions at 14
to 15 MeV on medium and heavy mass nuclei
(n,alpha). (see other Entries with the same ref.)
80% of neutrons have an energy of 14.7 +- 0.3 MeV
However, for measurements on Cr50, Fe54, Zn64, Mo92
and Au197, due to the sample thickness, the mean
energy of neutrons was around 14.6 +- 0.4 MeV.
High purity target in powder form.
Each value is based on four or more independent
unit employing two 12.5 * 7.5 cm NaI(Tl) crystals.
Ge(Li) detector, 28 cm3, connected to a 4000
